dispatcher_config.stop_tokens_note: Qwen stop tokens sent to Llama model. No template leakage observed — Ollama /api/chat handles template server-side. W2 should add Llama-native stop tokens (<|eot_id|>, <|end_of_text|>) for correctness.

integration_status: implicit — dispatcher is model-agnostic at /api/chat level; Llama dispatches without code changes

probe_a_identity.prompt: Respond with your model name and nothing else.
probe_a_identity.notes: Correct family name, no extra content, no template tokens

probe_b_structured.prompt: Return a JSON object with fields "name" (string), "count" (integer), "items" (list of strings). Use name="test", count=3, items=["a","b","c"]. Return only the JSON, no other text.
probe_b_structured.notes: Exact JSON, no wrapping, correct schema. Notably fast (0.71s vs Qwen 27.31s — 3B vs 9B parameter difference)

probe_c_language.prompt: Briefly describe the ACID properties of a database transaction. Respond in English only.
probe_c_language.raw_response_excerpt: ACID stands for Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, and Durability ...
probe_c_language.notes: English throughout, on-topic, no language drift

w2_concerns: Dispatcher sends Qwen stop tokens to Llama — no leakage observed because Ollama /api/chat applies template server-side, but W2 should add Llama-native stop tokens for correctness
Llama 3.x native stop tokens: <|eot_id|> (end of turn), <|end_of_text|> (end of text)
